title: Sour Flaky Thai Peach Dumplings
description: These crispy, glutenfree dumplings burst with tart peach, lime, and tamarind flavors, perfect for a comforting sickday snack.
instructions: In a bowl, combine diced peaches, rice flour, and tapioca flour; stir until peaches are evenly coated.

In a small saucepan, whisk lime juice, tamarind paste, fish sauce, brown sugar, chili flakes, and water over medium heat until sugar dissolves; simmer 1 minute, then remove from heat and cool slightly.
Pour the warm sauce over the peach mixture; mix gently until peaches are coated; set aside to marinate for 10 minutes.
Lay a dumpling wrapper on a clean surface; spoon 1 tablespoon of the peach filling onto the center; sprinkle a pinch of cilantro.
Fold the wrapper in half, then pleat the edges by pinching them together; press firmly to seal, forming a half‑moon shape.
Heat 1–2 inches of vegetable oil in a deep‑pan to 180°C (350°F); test by dropping a small piece of dough—it should sizzle and rise.
Carefully slide the dumplings into the hot oil in batches; fry 3–4 minutes per side, or until golden brown and crisp.
Remove dumplings with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels; drizzle with remaining sauce and a sprinkle of cilantro.
Serve hot, accompanied by a small bowl of lime‑y dipping sauce if desired.
